 {"id":"bd-1bje","title":"Implement mock layer for Rust QuickJS runtime","description":"# Implement mock layer for Rust QuickJS runtime

## Context
The Rust runtime executes extensions in QuickJS. The hostcall bridge (src/extensions.rs dispatch_hostcall_events/dispatch_hostcall_session) handles JS->Rust calls. For conformance testing, we need to intercept these and return mock responses from the shared mock spec.

## Architecture
In Rust, hostcalls flow:
1. JS calls pi.session(op, payload) or pi.events(op, payload)
2. QuickJS calls back to Rust via registered hostcall functions
3. Rust dispatch_hostcall_events() / dispatch_hostcall_session() routes to real implementations
4. For mocking: we need TestSession / TestDispatcher that reads mock spec

## What Already Exists
- NullSession in extension_dispatcher.rs: returns empty/default for all session calls
- TestSession in extension_dispatcher.rs: similar but for tests
- These are close to what we need but lack mock spec integration

## What To Do
1. Create ConformanceMockSession that implements ExtensionSession trait
2. ConformanceMockSession reads mock_spec.json and returns configured responses
3. Create ConformanceMockDispatcher for non-session hostcalls (exec, http, etc.)
4. Create a test function: load extension + run with mocks + capture output as JSON
5. Output format must EXACTLY match the TS harness output format

## Key Implementation Details
- JsExtensionSnapshot already captures registrations (tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers)
- We need to also capture hostcall invocations during load
- The mock layer goes between the extension and the real dispatch

## Acceptance Criteria
- ConformanceMockSession passes compilation
- Load hello.ts extension with mock session, capture registrations
- Output JSON matches TS harness format","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.492512632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:39:08.830943349Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bje","depends_on_id":"bd-1e1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.522833776Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bje","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:30.509182032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1bzn","title":"Session picker: delete sessions (Ctrl+D) using trash when available","description":"# Goal
Add legacy session deletion UX to the session picker.

# Legacy Spec
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/session.md`:
- In `/resume`, select a session and press `Ctrl+D`, then confirm.
- If `trash` CLI is available, use it to avoid permanent deletion.

# Required Behavior
- While in session picker:
  - `Ctrl+D` triggers a confirmation dialog.
  - On confirm:
    - prefer invoking `trash <file>` if `trash` is in PATH.
    - else delete the JSONL file directly (and remove from index).
  - Update the picker list immediately.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep deletion *non-destructive by default* by preferring trash.
- Provide clear feedback on failure.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session delete works and updates the picker list.
- [ ] Uses `trash` when available.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:24.524917111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:49.115866554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:51:59.095123066Z","close_reason":"Implemented session picker delete with Ctrl+D: added confirm_delete state to SessionPickerOverlay, delete confirmation flow (y/n/Esc), delete_session_file() method that uses trash CLI when available with fallback to direct removal, and updated help text to show Ctrl+D hint.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bzn","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bzn","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cd5","title":"Settings: persist changes to ~/.pi/agent/settings.json and/or .pi/settings.json","description":"# Goal
Implement persistence for settings changes made from `/settings`.

# Required Behavior (legacy)
- Global settings: `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`
- Project overrides: `.pi/settings.json`
- Merge semantics: project overrides global (nested objects merged).

# Deliverables
- Helper to:
  - load existing JSON (if present)
  - update only the intended fields (minimal diffs)
  - write atomically (temp file + rename) with appropriate permissions

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Writes are atomic and do not corrupt settings on crash.
- [ ] Project vs global selection is explicit (UI or default behavior).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:29.601188380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:37.214778940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:57:53.296290670Z","close_reason":"Completed: atomic settings patch helper","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cd5","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1chv","title":"Populate extension catalog entries","description":"# Goal
